b' Santos Effects, House \xe2\x80\x9cTriage\xe2\x80\x9d & The Madness of Ryan Routh
Hello, and welcome back to The Best & The Brightest, your daily dispatch on all things politics. It\xe2\x80\x99s foreign policy Tuesday. Tonight, a close read of would-be Trump assassin Ryan Routh\xe2\x80\x99s book on foreign affairs. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Hello, and welcome back to The Best & The Brightest, your daily dispatch on all things politics. It\xe2\x80\x99s foreign policy Tuesday and I\xe2\x80\x99m your host, Julia Ioffe .

Tonight, a close read of would-be Trump assassin Ryan Routh \xe2\x80\x99s book on foreign affairs. But first, a few notes from around the Puck media universe\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Puck turned three : Some of you have been with me and Puck since the very beginning, when I started sending a prelaunch newsletter in the summer of 2021 called \xe2\x80\x9cTomorrow Will Be Worse.\xe2\x80\x9d I am so, so thankful for your loyalty over these years.

  • Book recommendation : My dear, dear friend, Dr. Ayana Elizabeth Johnson , a marine biologist and an incredibly powerful voice on climate change, has a new book out today. What If We Get It Right? is a book that, unlike so many others, takes the optimistic view of our response to the climate. Its star-studded cast examines what we can do to solve the climate crisis across various industries, and how. And speaking of star-studded: Ayana\xe2\x80\x99s launch party/climate variety show , tonight at the Brooklyn Library, is co-hosted by Jason Sudeikis and Roy Wood Jr. (I am so upset that I had to cancel my train to New York due to a scheduling conflict.) Get the book, you won\xe2\x80\x99t regret it!

Now, here\xe2\x80\x99s Abby Livingston with the latest happenings on Capitol Hill, which is now finally back in session\xe2\x80\xa6
A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Seniors are feeling the true cost of drug price \xe2\x80\x9cnegotiations.\xe2\x80\x9d

Instead of saving money, some Medicare patients will pay more for medicines.

Others may not be able to get their medicines \xe2\x80\x93 89% of insurers and PBMs say they plan to reduce access to medicines in Medicare Part D because of the Inflation Reduction Act.

Higher costs and less access: Not what seniors were promised.
The Santos Butterfly Effect
Amid the current shutdown chatter engulfing Capitol Hill, one fascinating subplot is how, if Hakeem Jeffries and his colleagues end up flipping the House, more credit will be owed to the indicted fabulist George Santos than anyone in the current minority leaders\xe2\x80\x99 ranks. Here\xe2\x80\x99s the logic\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Souzzi\xe2\x80\x99s trailblazing : When Santos was ejected from Congress last November, it set the stage for a February special election in Long Island\xe2\x80\x99s 3rd District. Of course, special elections often establish the tone for the rest of the cycle, and that race, in particular, allowed both parties to test their messaging a full six months before the general election ad wars.

    Indeed, when Tom Suozzi won the Santos seat back for Democrats by a whopping 8 points, both parties agreed that he\xe2\x80\x99d cracked the code for how to handle Republican attacks on border security. To wit: Suozzi embraced a tough-on-the-border stance while blasting Republicans for doing Trump \xe2\x80\x99s bidding and killing the conservative Senate border plan. His playbook has since been deployed in Democratic ads across the country and, of course, by Kamala Harris in this month\xe2\x80\x99s debate. Right now, most sober House race obsessives believe the Democrats have the better hand to play, but that the battle for control of the House could still break in either direction.

    The big question over the next few weeks is whether the current Democratic House ad spending advantage will impact races\xe2\x80\x94and whether ad buys on either side get pulled. Often, ads get canceled and spending gets redirected when a party feels confident that a race has been put to bed; other times, they\xe2\x80\x99re pulled from one campaign to shore up incumbents elsewhere in a grim tactic called \xe2\x80\x9ctriage.\xe2\x80\x9d

  • The $400M seat : Meanwhile, on the Senate front, Ad Impact Politics just put out a truly mind-bending report projecting that TV ad spending on the Ohio Senate race alone will be in the $400 million range. That\xe2\x80\x99s nearly half a billion dollars for a single Senate seat. But this matchup, between Democrat Sherrod Brown and Republican Bernie Moreno , still ranks second to the Montana Senate race on the list of the political class\xe2\x80\x99s down-ballot fixations. Whichever party wins that race will probably control the Senate.

    In any case, that $400 million figure does not include direct mail expenditures, costs for polling, voter mobilization, etcetera. This is more than the G.D.P. of most countries . And while there is scant public polling on this race, most handicappers rate it as a pure tossup. And while I\xe2\x80\x99m hearing concerns about potential ad inventory shortages in places including Ohio, operatives I\xe2\x80\x99ve spoken with remain confident that local affiliates in busy markets will find more TV ad minutes using well-oiled tricks of the trade, such as extending the 11 o\xe2\x80\x99clock news by a segment or two for additional commercial breaks.
The Madness of Ryan Routh
A close reading of the wannabe Trump assassin\xe2\x80\x99s self-published memoir, documenting his time in Ukraine, reveals a mentally ill man grappling with the existential comedown from his delusions of grandeur.
JULIA IOFFE
On Sunday afternoon, yet another maniac with a gun targeted Donald Trump , this time from the bushes of the Trump International Golf Club in West Palm Beach. The news\xe2\x80\x94a second assassination attempt in as many months\xe2\x80\x94triggered all the predictable reactions: relief on the left that the man, Ryan Routh , lacked any clear or consistent political ideology; accusations from the right that Democrats\xe2\x80\x99 anti-Trump rhetoric had inspired the would-be shooter; and endless condemnations and think-pieces about the return of political violence in America.

What I found more surprising (and interesting), as I\xe2\x80\x99m sure many people did, were Routh\xe2\x80\x99s ties to Ukraine. The picture that\xe2\x80\x99s emerged so far is of a wannabe legionnaire with delusions of grandeur. Routh had told people he was willing to fight and die for Ukraine; that he traveled to the country and, while he failed to register for service\xe2\x80\x94he was then 56 and had no combat or even military experience\xe2\x80\x94he apparently tried to recruit Afghans to join the cause. The Times \xe2\x80\x99s Thomas Gibbons-Neff , who spoke to Routh in 2023, found him totally weird and untethered from reality. \xe2\x80\x9cIt was clear he was in way over his head,\xe2\x80\x9d Gibbons-Neff wrote this week.

But the most interesting reporting came from the Associated Press, which discovered Routh\xe2\x80\x99s self-published memoir on Amazon. The 300-page book, still available on Kindle, is called Ukraine\xe2\x80\x99s Unwinnable War: The Fatal Flaw of Democracy, World Abandonment and the Global Citizen\xe2\x80\x94Taiwan, Afghanistan, North Korea and the End of Humanity . I have read it so you don\xe2\x80\x99t have to.

As its title implies, it is not just about Ukraine, but about world affairs as a whole. It is the kind of book written by an intensely interested and passionate amateur observer of geopolitics; the kind of person who is first in line at a Politics & Prose book event to offer a long, rambling, mildly paranoid treatise masquerading as a question.
A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Seniors are feeling the true cost of drug price \xe2\x80\x9cnegotiations.\xe2\x80\x9d

Instead of saving money, some Medicare patients will pay more for medicines.

Others may not be able to get their medicines \xe2\x80\x93 89% of insurers and PBMs say they plan to reduce access to medicines in Medicare Part D because of the Inflation Reduction Act.

Higher costs and less access: Not what seniors were promised.
Deluded & Disillusioned
When it was reported that Routh had once declared his desire to fight for Ukraine, there was a fear among the country\xe2\x80\x99s many supporters in D.C. that Republicans might seize on his affinity for the country to cast further doubt on America\xe2\x80\x99s own war efforts. They still might, of course\xe2\x80\x94MAGA doesn\xe2\x80\x99t really need factual evidence to definitively declare something true\xe2\x80\x94but Routh\xe2\x80\x99s book doesn\xe2\x80\x99t provide much material to work with. If anything, the volume reveals a man who is deeply disillusioned by Ukraine, who traveled to far Eastern Europe to aid in the ultimate battle between good and evil, but found a country that was, in his view, corrupt, arrogant, and ungrateful.

Routh\xe2\x80\x99s journey began with the loftiest of ideals. \xe2\x80\x9cI just like every other good human with a moral compass knew that the right thing to do was to go to Ukraine and fight for freedom and human rights, and that is what I did,\xe2\x80\x9d he wrote in Chapter 5\xe2\x80\x94which, somehow, is the first chapter of the book, before switching to Chapter 1. (I have reproduced Routh\xe2\x80\x99s disjointed orthography.) Routh wrote that it took him a month to \xe2\x80\x9cwrap up business\xe2\x80\x9d in the U.S., put all of his belongings in storage, buy military gear, and set off for Ukraine. He arrived in Kyiv, via Poland, in the first months of the war, when the Russian military was approaching the city gates. Strangely, Routh was surprised to find the capital a ghost town.

After locating a recruitment post, Routh was, he claims, \xe2\x80\x9creluctantly\xe2\x80\x9d turned away by the Ukrainian military despite his utter lack of any experience that would be of value to them. According to his account, he then set about attempting to recruit foreign fighters for Ukraine\xe2\x80\x99s war effort\xe2\x80\x94in, of all places, Kyiv. Routh writes that during this time, he frequently found himself a fish out of water, plucked from the seas of his delusions. Repeatedly falling back on grandiose bromides\xe2\x80\x94\xe2\x80\x9cWe are the forefathers of the future and we must shape it the way that we wish it to be\xe2\x80\x9d\xe2\x80\x94Routh clearly finds the real world to be a bewildering and disappointing place.

According to the book, Routh harassed various government offices into letting him set up a tent in Kyiv\xe2\x80\x99s Independence Square (the famous Maidan) to act as a volunteer center. He set up a blue tarp with \xe2\x80\x9cALL GLOBAL CITIZENS COME STAND WITH UKRAINIANS NOW!\xe2\x80\x9d emblazoned in yellow letters\xe2\x80\x94but, he writes, his ostensible deal with city hall was merely a verbal agreement, one the police did not recognize or respect. They \xe2\x80\x9cballed up\xe2\x80\x9d the tent and Routh was forced to descend back into the Kyiv metro, which was where he regularly retreated to make more signs. Unsurprisingly to this reader, but to Routh\xe2\x80\x99s great shock, he found that recruiting foreign fighters in the Ukrainian capital was like going fishing in a sandbox. He was also shocked to discover that his display of flags representing the countries from which volunteers had come to join Ukraine\xe2\x80\x99s military proved controversial with locals. He quickly found himself focused on maintaining the integrity of his flag display, guarding it from vandals on the one hand and the police on the other.

He was most shocked, however, that Ukrainians weren\xe2\x80\x99t massively grateful to him and his efforts. One woman, outraged at Routh\xe2\x80\x99s focus on foreigners, cut down all his flags and replaced them with one big Ukrainian one. \xe2\x80\x9cI asked her what message did that send\xe2\x80\x94that she and Ukraine did not want any outside support or help?\xe2\x80\x9d Routh recalled, clearly not understanding the woman\xe2\x80\x99s point, that it was Ukrainians, not a handful of foreign fighters, who were doing most of the fighting and dying. (\xe2\x80\x9cDoing this I recognized that I must also recognize the Ukrainians that have also died in the war,\xe2\x80\x9d he reluctantly admits later.)

His monument to foreign fighters destroyed, the police sent him on a bureaucratic wild goose chase to file charges, a quest Routh eventually decided to abandon in favor of\xe2\x80\xa6 building drones. Like everyone else, he quickly understood that drones were a key weapons system in this war, which became a vast testing ground for the latest UAV technology. \xe2\x80\x9cWe were able to assemble a team of engineers and builders to work together to build drones for the army,\xe2\x80\x9d Routh writes, rather implausibly. \xe2\x80\x9cWe had engineers from England, Azerbaijan, Iran, Ukrainian [ sic ], USA and seemingly an unlimited network of support and a million ideas swirled in a million directions and when it came time to buy materials all of the brilliant minds and motivation fell flat.\xe2\x80\x9d

Routh, who calls himself \xe2\x80\x9cthe unofficial project manager\xe2\x80\x9d of this alleged drone workshop, blames himself. \xe2\x80\x9cI was unable to harness all of the assets and abilities assembled to guide the collective unit to some sort of solution,\xe2\x80\x9d he writes, despite the fact that he \xe2\x80\x9ccaressed and cajoled every partner\xe2\x80\x9d and \xe2\x80\x9cwould have ended up with 10 or 20 different drones and yet I ended up with nothing.\xe2\x80\x9d Undeterred, Routh writes that he tried to build his own drone, but found it \xe2\x80\x9cimpossible to source\xe2\x80\x9d the necessary electronics and circuitry. When he cobbled one together (or says he does), he found that Ukrainian authorities wouldn\xe2\x80\x99t let him test his drone\xe2\x80\x94not in Kyiv, nor in Mykolaiv, close to the front lines. He cannot comprehend why and abandons his drone on the scrap heap.
From Kyiv to Palm Beach
Thus ends Routh\xe2\x80\x99s ego trip to Ukraine, which, he admits, was an abject failure\xe2\x80\x94as was he, personally. \xe2\x80\x9cI am the failure, the hypocrite, the loser that wants the world to change but let communism beat me down and exhaust me and send me home,\xe2\x80\x9d he writes. \xe2\x80\x9cI personally have failed humanity and wasted 5 months with no measurable success.\xe2\x80\x9d No less humbly, he adds: \xe2\x80\x9cIn the war between myself and Ukraine, where I had hoped that I could bend Ukraine towards democracy and freedom and human rights and be a representative of the USA, I lost the fight. It was a childish idealistic endeavor that was unwinnable.\xe2\x80\x9d

But it is not just his fault, he concludes. It is also Ukraine\xe2\x80\x99s, which is why he returns home with such bitterness and resentment of the place. On one hand, he acknowledges that Ukraine is fighting on the side of good for the sake of the entire world, but he also feels that Ukrainian society is deeply cynical and still Communist. Ukrainians, Routh gripes, always wanted to know \xe2\x80\x9cWho is paying you?\xe2\x80\x9d Given the spy games afoot there, it is not an unreasonable question. But in it, Routh discovers Eastern European distrust of strangers and their motives\xe2\x80\x94and is horrified by it. \xe2\x80\x9cWhat type of childhood must that be like growing up where everything has a price?\xe2\x80\x9d he wonders. \xe2\x80\x9cIs the simple act of love and caring omitted from day to day life as there is no mechanism for reimbursement?\xe2\x80\x9d At the same time, he is angered by Ukrainians\xe2\x80\x99 \xe2\x80\x9clack of appreciation for those that pay to travel to Ukraine and risk death to fight for their freedom.\xe2\x80\x9d Foreigners, he writes, \xe2\x80\x9cexpend great sums of money\xe2\x80\x9d but aren\xe2\x80\x99t feted as heroes. The irony of his complaint about Ukrainians\xe2\x80\x99 \xe2\x80\x9ctransactional\xe2\x80\x9d nature is lost on him entirely.

Ukraine, he feels, is unable but also unwilling to win the war, in part because the conflict is an excuse to milk the West for cash. At the same time, he blames the West for not giving Ukraine enough resources to win, and says the U.S. is not doing enough to counter the threats from the dictatorships of the world. \xe2\x80\x9cWe should encircle Taiwan with military ships\xe2\x80\x9d lest the U.S. be seen as serving Taiwan up to Beijing \xe2\x80\x9con a silver platter,\xe2\x80\x9d he writes. The solution to every problem is American power, Routh declares\xe2\x80\x94\xe2\x80\x9cWe should be the peacekeepers and the policemen for the world\xe2\x80\x9d\xe2\x80\x94while simultaneously condemning American defense spending as a waste of resources and \xe2\x80\x9cstupid.\xe2\x80\x9d

The far right might seize on what they perceive as Routh\xe2\x80\x99s passionate Ukraine advocacy, using it either to paint Routh as a Democrat or to further drive their base\xe2\x80\x99s animosity toward Kyiv. But to read Routh\xe2\x80\x99s book is to see a very different picture of him than the one painted in the media. It is tempting to call his foreign policy views confused\xe2\x80\x94which they are\xe2\x80\x94but the far more relevant conclusion is that he is mentally ill. Despite his overconfidence and self-aggrandizement, he is clearly unable to understand the world around him. He finds it baffling, infuriating, and, well, incoherent.
That\xe2\x80\x99s all for me this week, friends. I\xe2\x80\x99ll catch you back here next week. Until then, good night. Tomorrow will be worse.

Julia
